articles.title: COLLABORATIVE AI FOR PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT MASTERCLASS
articles.summary: On November 4-6, Miro will be running an exclusive masterclass series [4]. Over 1,000 product leaders have already signed up - save your spot [4] for free access to all three sessions:

articles.title: IS AI ADOPTION SLOWING DOWN?
articles.summary: AI adoption is slowing, but product stickiness is rising. As social media becomes a last-resort business model and job seekers resist AI résumé filters, it's clear that lasting growth still depends on human judgment.

articles.title: IN THE AIR
articles.summary: This year's Coalesce felt like the end of an era, with the venture capitalists - and the raw sense of possibility they embodied - now gone. The data world has matured into stability, leaving its pioneers to choose between the rush of invention and the calm of permanence.

articles.title: MOMENTUM IS THE ONLY MOAT
articles.summary: Real moats like brand, network effects, and scale only form once a startup gains traction, so in the early days, momentum isn't the moat - it's the engine that builds one.

articles.title: HOW TO BE LEGITIMATE
articles.summary: Legitimacy in tech has evolved from user groups and magazine reviews to venture capital and AI credentials. Despite new tools, true success still depends on earning trust and demonstrating genuine expertise - not just chasing trends.

articles.title: VIBE CODING STOPS AT THE PROTOTYPE LEVEL. THIS STARTUP IS LEADING THE WAY ON VIBE SOLUTIONING
articles.summary: Vibe coding tools are great for delivering prototypes, but not apps that work at scale. Fresh off a $15M seed round, Rocket [9] is pioneering _vibe solutioning_: AI that builds production-ready frontend, backend, data flows, and more. Explore Rocket [9] to turn your ideas or Figma designs into production apps.

articles.title: 4 STEPS TO BUILDING A PRODUCT STRATEGY
articles.summary: This post outlines a four-step approach to building product strategy: gather inputs, craft strategic narratives, find leverage, and make focused choices. Strategy isn't neat or formulaic - it's an ongoing, messy process driven by learning, conviction, and disciplined focus.

articles.title: THE ONE-DAY PRODUCT LOOP
articles.summary: Grant Lee's team at Gamma prototypes, tests, and refines ideas within a single day using AI tools and real user feedback. It's a model of instant learning and iteration that shows how fast, data-driven product development is becoming the new standard.

articles.title: THE FEASIBILITY FALLACY: WHY TEAMS STILL TRIP OVER TECHNICAL FEASIBILITY
articles.summary: Technical feasibility is the hidden backbone of great product teams. When treated as a guide instead of a hurdle, it keeps ambition honest, reduces rework, and turns speed into sustainable progress.

articles.title: 5 TIPS TO SUCCEED WITH STAKEHOLDER MANAGEMENT
articles.summary: Prioritize key players, build trust, align on shared outcomes, involve stakeholders early, and manage conflict constructively. Done well, these practices turn stakeholder alignment into a lasting advantage for great product leadership.

articles.title: CRAFT IS SCARCE
articles.summary: AI makes it easy to build fast, but true value now lies in the 20% of craft that turns rapid output into thoughtful, user-centered design.

articles.title: ADVANCED TIME MANAGEMENT PRINCIPLES
articles.summary: Many product leaders avoid strategic thinking out of fear or “placebo productivity.”
